title: Convert units (metrics)
description: Convert between metric measures of distance, volume, and mass.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert $7{,}348$ grams to kilograms.**

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilograms

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ kilogram} ={1{,}000}\text{ grams}$

So, $1 \text{ gram} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ kilogram}$.
$7{,}348$ grams is equivalent to ${7.348}$ kilograms.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert $475$ milliliters to liters.**

[[☃ input-number 1]] liters

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ liter} ={1{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$

So, $1 \text{ milliliter} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ liter}$

$\begin {align}{475 \text{ mL}}&= {475 \text{ 
mL}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ L}}{{1{,}000 \text{ mL}}} 
\\\\
&= {\dfrac{475 \cancel{\text{ mL}}}{1}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ L}}{{1{,}000 \cancel{\text{ mL}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{475\text{ L} }{1{,}000}\\\\
&=475\text{ L}\div1{,}000
\end{align}$
$475$ milliliters is equivalent to ${0.475}$ liters.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Arrange the following measurements in order from smallest to largest.** 


[[☃ sorter 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ liter} ={1{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$

So, $1 \text{ milliliter} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ liter}$
We know $\greenD{2.1}$ liters is less than $\maroonD{3}$ liters.  But where does $\blueD{32{,}000}$ milliliters fit?

We need to convert $\blueD{32{,}000}$ milliliters to liters before we can compare.
$\begin {align}{\blueD{32{,}000} \text{ mL}}&= {\blueD{32{,}000} \text{ 
mL}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ L}}{{1{,}000 \text{ mL}}} 
\\\\
&= {\dfrac{\blueD{32{,}000} \cancel{\text{ mL}}}{1}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ L}}{{1{,}000 \cancel{\text{ mL}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{32{,}000}\text{ L} }{1{,}000}\\\\
&=\blueD{32{,}000}\text{ L}\div1{,}000\\\\
&=\blueD{32}\text{ L}
\end{align}$
We can order the measurements written in liters from smallest to largest as follows:  

$\greenD{2.1}$ liters  
$\maroonD{3}$ liters  
$\blueD{32}$ liters  
So the original measurements in order from smallest to largest are as follows:  

$\greenD{2.1}$ liters  
$\maroonD{3}$ liters  
$\blueD{32{,}000}$ milliliters

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Arrange the following measurements in order from smallest to largest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ liter} ={1{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$

So, $1 \text{ milliliter} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ liter}$
We know $\maroonD{0.7}$ liters is less than $\greenD{4.1}$ liters.  But where does $\blueD{2{,}000}$ milliliters fit?

We need to convert $\blueD{2{,}000}$ milliliters to liters before we can compare.
$\begin {align}{\blueD{2{,}000} \text{ mL}}&= {\blueD{2{,}000} \text{ 
mL}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ L}}{{1{,}000 \text{ mL}}} 
\\\\
&= {\dfrac{\blueD{2{,}000} \cancel{\text{ mL}}}{1}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ L}}{{1{,}000 \cancel{\text{ mL}}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{2{,}000}\text{ L} }{1{,}000}\\\\
&=\blueD{2{,}000}\text{ L}\div1{,}000\\\\
&=\blueD{2}\text{ L}
\end{align}$



We can order the measurements written in liters from smallest to largest as follows:  

$\maroonD{0.7}$ liters  
$\blueD{2}$ liters  
$\greenD{4.1}$ liters
So the original measurements in order from smallest to largest are as follows:  

$\maroonD{0.7}$ liters  
$\blueD{2{,}000}$ milliliters  
$\greenD{4.1}$ liters

assessment_items.question_content:
**Arrange the following measurements in order from smallest to largest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ kilogram} ={1{,}000}\text{ grams}$

So, $1 \text{ gram} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ kilogram}$.
We know $\maroonD{15}$ grams is less than $\greenD{55}$ grams.  But where does $\blueD{0.005}$ kilograms fit?

We need to convert $\blueD{0.005}$ kilograms to grams before we can compare.
$\begin{align}
\blueD{0.005}\text{ kg} &= \blueD{0.005}\text{ kg} \times 1{,}000\text{ g per kg}\\\\
&=\blueD5\text{ g}
\end{align}$

We can order the measurements written in grams from smallest to largest as follows:  

$\blueD{5}$ grams  
$\maroonD{15}$ grams  
$\greenD{55}$ grams  
So the original measurements in order from smallest to largest are as follows:  

$\blueD{0.005}$ kilograms  
$\maroonD{15}$ grams  
$\greenD{55}$ grams

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measurements is greater?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ meter} = {100}\text{ centimeters}$

So, $1 \text{ centimeter} = \dfrac{1}{100}\text{ meter}$.
We need to convert $\blueD{1{,}300}$ centimeters to meters before we can compare.

$ \blueD{1{,}300}  \text{ cm}= \dfrac{\blueD{1{,}300}}{{100}}= \blueD{1{,}300}\div{100}=\blueD{13}\text{ m}$
We can order the measurements written in meters from smallest to largest as follows:  

$\maroonD{5}$ meter  
$\blueD{13}$ meters  

$\blueD{1{,}300}$ centimeters is the greater measurement.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measurements is greater?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ kilogram} ={1{,}000}\text{ grams}$

So, $1 \text{ gram} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ kilogram}$.
We need to convert $\blueD{4{,}500}$ grams to kilograms before we can compare.


$\begin {align}{\blueD{4{,}500} \text{ g}}&= {{\blueD{4{,}500} \text{ 
g}}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ kg}}{{1{,}000 \text{ g}}} 
\\\\
&= {\dfrac{\blueD{4{,}500} \cancel{\text{ g}}}{1}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ kg}}{{1{,}000 \cancel{\text{ g}}}} 
\\\\
&= \dfrac{{\blueD{4{,}500} \text{ kg}}}{{1{,}000}} 
\\\\
&= {\blueD{4{,}500} \text{ kg}}\div{1{,}000}
\\\\
&={\blueD{4.5}\text{ kg}}\end{align}$
We can order the measurements written in kilograms from smallest to largest as follows:  

$\maroonD{0.8}$ kilogram  
$\blueD{4.5}$ kilograms  
$\blueD{4{,}500}$ grams is the greater measurement.

options.explanation:

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measurements is greater?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ liter} ={1{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$

So, $1 \text{ milliliter} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ liter}$
We need to convert $\blueD{0.5}$ liters to milliliters before we can compare.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{0.5}\text{ L} &= \blueD{0.5}\text{ L} \times 1{,}000\text{ mL per L}\\\\
&=\blueD{500}\text{ mL}
\end{align}$
We can order the measurements written in milliliters from smallest to largest as follows:  

$\maroonD{400}$ milliliters      
$\blueD{500}$ milliliters  
$\blueD{0.5}$ liters is the greater measurement.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the table to convert the units of measure from kilometers to meters or meters to kilometers.**

Meters | Kilometers
:-: | :-:
$1{,}000$ |  $1$
$13$ | [[☃ input-number 2]]
[[☃ input-number 4]] | $47$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ kilometer} ={1{,}000}\text{ meters}$

So, $1 \text{ meter} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ kilometers}$.
First, let's convert ${13}$ meters  to kilometers.

$\begin {align}{13 \text{ m}}&= {{13 \text{ 
m}}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ km}}{{1{,}000 \text{ m}}} 
\\\\
&= {\dfrac{13 \cancel{\text{ m}}}{1}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ km}}{{1{,}000 \cancel{\text{ m}}}} 
\\\\
&= \dfrac{{13 \text{ km}}}{{1{,}000}} 
\\\\
&= {13 \text{ km}}\div{1{,}000}\\\\
&=0.013\text{ km}
\end{align}$
Now, let's see how many meters are in ${47}$ kilometers.

$\begin{align}
{47}\text{ km}&={47\text{ km}}\times{1{,}000\text{ m per km}}\\\\
&={47{,}000}\text{ m}
\end{align}$
Here is the completed table:

Meters | Kilometers
:-: | :-:
$1{,}000$ |  $1$
$13$ | $\blueD{0.013}$
$\maroonD{47{,}000}$ | $47$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeters| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the table to convert the units of measure from kilometers to meters or meters to kilometers.**

Meters | Kilometers
:-: | :-:
$1{,}000$ |  $1$
$15{,}000$ | [[☃ input-number 2]]
[[☃ input-number 4]] | $0.86$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
$1 \text{ kilometer} ={1{,}000}\text{ meters}$

So, $1 \text{ meter} = \dfrac1{1{,}000}\text{ kilometers}$.
First, let's convert ${15{,}000}$ meters to kilometers.

$\begin {align}{15{,}000 \text{ m}}&= {{15{,}000 \text{ 
m}}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ km}}{{1{,}000 \text{ m}}} 
\\\\
&= {\dfrac{15{,}000 \cancel{\text{ m}}}{1}}\times \dfrac{1 \text{ km}}{{1{,}000 \cancel{\text{ m}}}} 
\\\\
&= \dfrac{{15{,}000 \text{ km}}}{{1{,}000}} 
\\\\
&= {15{,}000 \text{ km}}\div{1{,}000}\\\\
&=15\text{ km}
\end{align}$
Now, let's see how many meters are in ${0.86}$ kilometers.

$\begin{align}
{0.86}\text{ km}&={0.86\text{ km}}\times{1{,}000\text{ m per km}}\\\\
&={860}\text{ m}
\end{align}$
Here is the completed table:

Meters | Kilometers
:-: | :-:
$1{,}000$ |  $1$
$15{,}000$ | $\blueD{15}$
$\maroonD{860}$ | $0.86$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
